devanagari: अघासुरवधः । श्रीशुक उवाच । क्वचिद्वनाशाय मनो दधद् व्रजात् । प्रातः समुत्थाय वयस्यवत्सपान् । प्रबोधयन् श्रृंगरवेण चारुणा । विनिर्गतो वत्...

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
One day, intending to have his break-fast (first meal) in the woods, Hari got up early in the morning and woke up his fellow cowherd boys (tending the calves) by the charming sound of his horn, and proceeded from Vraja with calves going in front.

devanagari: तेनैव साकं पृथुकाः सहस्रशः । स्निग्धाः सुशिग्वेत्रविषाणवेणवः । स्वान् स्वान् सहस्रो परिसङ्ख्ययान्वितान् । वत्सान् पुरस्कृत्य विनिर्ययुर...

translation: Immediately, at that clarion-call rushed forth joyfully thousands[1] of affectionate young boys simultaneously, equipping themselves with slings, sticks, horns and flutes, each driving his own herd of thousands of calves.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Innumerable—?at?ānāṃ bālānāṃ ca asaṅkhyayatvam uktam—Bhāgavata Candrikā, VC.

devanagari: कृष्णवत्सैः असङ्ख्यातैः यूथीकृत्य स्ववत्सकान् । चारयन्तोऽर्भलीलाभिः विजह्रुः तत्र तत्र ह ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇavatsaiḥ asaṅkhyātaiḥ yūthīkṛtya svavatsakān cārayanto

translation: It is traditionally reported that making their own calves join the herd of innumerable calves of Kṛṣṇa, and grazing them, they diverted themselves in children’s games at various places.

devanagari: फलप्रबालस्तवक सुमनःपिच्छधातुभिः । काचगुञ्जामणिस्वर्णभूषिता अप्यभूषयन् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: phalaprabālastavaka sumanaḥpicchadhātubhiḥ kācaguñjāmaṇisvarṇabhūṣitā apyabhūṣayan
translation: Although they were already adorned (by their mothers) with glass-beads, Guñjā seeds, precious stones and gold ornaments, they decorated themselves with fruits, tender foliage, bunches of flowers, feathers and coloured earths.

devanagari: मुष्णन्तोऽन्योन्य शिक्यादीन् न्ज्ञातानाराच्च चिक्षिपुः । तत्रत्याश्च पुनर्दूरात् हसन्तश्च पुनर्ददुः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: muṣṇanto tatratyāśca punardūrāt hasantaśca punardaduḥ

translation: They stole the slings and other objects belonging to each other, and when detected, they threw them at a distance. Boys who were sitting there threw them still further laughing all the while, returning them (to the respective owners when they were on the point of shedding tears).

devanagari: यदि दूरं गतः कृष्णो वनशोभेक्षणाय तम् । अहं पूर्वं अहं पूर्वं इति संस्पृश्य रेमिरे ॥ ६ ॥

translation: If Kṛṣṇa had gone to a distance to observe the beauty of the forest, they vied with each other in touching Kṛṣṇa first and enjoyed themselves (in this competition).

devanagari: केचिद् वेणून् वादयन्तो ध्मान्तः शृङ्गाणि केचन । केचिद् भृंङ्गैः प्रगायन्तः कूजन्तः कोकिलैः परे ॥ ७ ॥

translation: Some played on the flutes; some blew their horns, some sang humming (hummed) in tune with black-bees, some imitated the sweet notes of the cuckoo.

devanagari: विच्छायाभिः प्रधावन्तो गच्छन्तः साधुहंसकैः । बकैः उपविशन्तश्च नृत्यन्तश्च कलापिभिः ॥ ८ ॥

translation: Some ran with the shadows of the (flying) birds; some walked beautifully like royal swans; some sat down with the cranes, while others danced with peacocks.

devanagari: विकर्षन्तः कीशबालान् आरोहन्तश्च तैर्द्रुमान् । विकुर्वन्तश्च तैः साकं प्लवन्तश्च पलाशिषु ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vikarṣantaḥ kīśabālān ārohantaśca tairdrumān vikurvantaśca taiḥ sākaṃ plavantaśca palāśiṣu

translation: Some pulled monkey’s tails dangling down from branches of trees, while others climbed trees along with the tails in hand. Some were making wry faces at monkeys while others were jumping from one branch to another (like monkeys).

devanagari: साकं भेकैर्विलङ्घन्तः सरित् प्रस्रवसम्प्लुताः । विहसन्तः प्रतिच्छायाः शपन्तश्च प्रतिस्वनान् ॥ १० ॥

translation: Hopping with frogs, some became drenched with the waters of the streams and waterfalls; some were laughing at their own shadows (while making faces at one’s own reflection), while others cursed their own echoes.

devanagari: इत्थं सतां ब्रह्मसुखानुभूत्या । दास्यं गतानां परदैवतेन । मायाश्रितानां नरदारकेण । साकं विजह्रुः कृतपुण्यपुञ्जाः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ satāṃ brahmasukhānubhūtyā dāsyaṃ gatānāṃ paradaivatena māyāśritānāṃ naradārakeṇa sākaṃ vijahruḥ kṛtapuṇyapuñjāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ das ], [accusative single from √ das ]

translation: In this way, the cowherd boys who amassed a vast fund of merits (in the past), played with Kṛṣṇa who was to the knowers the veritable Brahman, with all the realization of the absolute bliss, the Supreme Deity to the devotees who dedicated themselves to his service, and a human child to those who are under the influence of Māyā or Ignorance.

devanagari: यत्पादपांसुः बहुजन्मकृच्छ्रतो । धृतात्मभिः योगिभिरप्यलभ्यः । स एव यद्दृग्विषयः स्वयं स्थितः । किं वर्ण्यते दिष्टमतो व्रजौकसाम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yatpādapāṃsuḥ bahujanmakṛcchrato dhṛtātmabhiḥ yogibhirapyalabhyaḥ sa eva yaddṛgviṣayaḥ svayaṃ sthitaḥ kiṃ varṇyate diṣṭamato vrajaukasām

translation: Is it possible to describe the blessedness and good fortune of the inhabitants of Vraja unto whose ken of perception he presented himself in person? The Lord, the dust of whose feet cannot be obtained (and attained to) by the selfcontrolled yogis who have performed austerities for many past lives.

devanagari: अथ अघनामाभ्यपतन् महासुरः । तेषां सुखक्रीडनवीक्षणाक्षमः । नित्यं यदन्तर्निजजीवितेप्सुभिः । पीतामृतैः अप्यमरैः प्रतीक्ष्यते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: atha aghanāmābhyapatan mahāsuraḥ teṣāṃ sukhakrīḍanavīkṣaṇākṣamaḥ nityaṃ yadantarnijajīvitepsubhiḥ pītāmṛtaiḥ apyamaraiḥ pratīkṣyate

[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Now (on one occasion), a powerful demon named Agha who could not tolerate the sight of their happy games, attacked the demon whose vulnerable point i.e. death was eagerly watched over even by immortals who had been anxious to safeguard their lives (from him), even though they assimilated (drunk) nectar (and hence had no fear from death).[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Or Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: The sight of their happy games which even gods- who have drunk nectar are anxious to realize in their hearts, to sustain their life thereby.
Life is not really fruitful by drinking nectar but by drinking the ambrosia of the Lord’s name and by remembering and contemplating the sports of the glorious Lord.
KD.: Gods who attained immortality by drinking nectar wish to take a birth as mortals on the earth, and participate in singing the eulogy of Kṛṣṇa’s exploits.
VT. takes amara as ‘Liberated beings who, being free from gross and subtle bodies, are immortal’. Though they have imbibed amṛta or Mokṣa, they desire to take a Līlāvigraha (sportive body) to participate in Hari’s sport.
According to Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇavism, a soul doesnot lose his individuality in the Mokṣa stage.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वार्भकान् कृष्णमुखान् अघासुरः । कंसानुशिष्टः स बकीबकानुजः । अयं तु मे सोदरनाशकृत्तयोरः । द्वयोर्ममैनं सबलं हनिष्ये ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvārbhakān kṛṣṇamukhān aghāsuraḥ kaṃsānuśiṣṭaḥ sa bakībakānujaḥ ayaṃ tu me sodaranāśakṛttayoraḥ dvayormamainaṃ sabalaṃ haniṣye

translation: Seeing the Gopa children of whom Kṛṣṇa was the leader, the demon Agha, the younger brother of Bakī (Pūtanā) and Baka (the demon killed by Kṛṣṇa), who was deputed by Kaṃsa (to kill Kṛṣṇa) said to himself, “This is the slayer of my brother and sister. To avenge them both I shall now kill Kṛṣṇa along with his army (of cowherd boys).

devanagari: एते यदा मत्सुहृदोस्तिलापः । कृतास्तदा नष्टसमा व्रजौकसः । प्राणे गते वर्ष्मसु का नु चिन्ता । प्रजासवः प्राणभृतो हि ये ते ॥ १५ ॥

translation: When these (Kṛṣṇa and his cowherd companions) are killed (and thus are made as if propitiatory libations of sesamum seeds and water to my departed brother and sister), all the residents of Vraja are as good as dead. Why worry about the Physical body when the vital breath (life) is departed? And all those who live (as human beings) have (i.e. consider) their children as their very life.”

devanagari: इति व्यवस्याजगरं बृहद् वपुः । स योजनायाम महाद्रिपीवरम् । धृत्वाद्‍भुतं व्यात्तगुहाननं तदा । पथि व्यशेत ग्रसनाशया खलः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vyavasyājagaraṃ bṛhad vapuḥ sa yojanāyāma mahādripīvaram dhṛtvād‍bhutaṃ vyāttaguhānanaṃ tadā pathi vyaśeta grasanāśayā khalaḥ

translation: Having made up his mind thus, wicked demon assumed a gigantic body of a boa-constrictor one yojana (eight miles) in length and stout like a big mountain, and with a desire to swallow them all, he lay motionless on their path, with his flabbergasting cavelike mouth kept wide open.

devanagari: धराधरोष्ठो जलदोत्तरोष्ठो । दर्याननान्तो गिरिशृङ्गदंष्ट्रः । ध्वान्तान्तरास्यो वितताध्वजिह्वः । परुषानिलश्वासदवेक्षणोष्णः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: dharādharoṣṭho jaladottaroṣṭho daryānanānto giriśṛṅgadaṃṣṭraḥ dhvāntāntarāsyo vitatādhvajihvaḥ paruṣānilaśvāsadavekṣaṇoṣṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: His lower lip lay on the earth, while the upper lip touched the clouds; the mouth between them appeared like a vale; his fangs were like summits of mountains, and the interior Part of his mouth was full of darkness; his tongue was like a broad road; his breath was like a tempestuous blast, while his enraged red hot eyes were (scorching) like a forest-conflagration.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा तं तादृशं सर्वे मत्वा वृन्दावनश्रियम् । व्यात्ताजगरतुण्डेन ह्युत्प्रेक्षन्ते स्म लीलया ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā taṃ tādṛśaṃ sarve matvā vṛndāvanaśriyam vyāttājagaratuṇḍena hyutprekṣante sma līlayā
translation: Beholding him in that form, all regarded it as a beautiful landscape of Vṛndāvana. And in a sportive mood they fancied its semblance to the open mouth of a boaconstrictor.

devanagari: अहो मित्राणि गदत सत्त्वकूटं पुरः स्थितम् । अस्मत्सङ्ग्रसनव्यात्त व्यालतुण्डायते न वा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: aho mitrāṇi gadata sattvakūṭaṃ puraḥ sthitam asmatsaṅgrasanavyātta vyālatuṇḍāyate na vā

translation: (The children began to talk about it as follows: “Ah! How wonderful I Tell us friends, if this animal like formation (living mountain) standing before us, does or does not resemble the open mouth of a gigantic wicked serpent anxious to devour us all?”

devanagari: सत्यमर्ककरारक्तं उत्तराहनुवद्‍घनम् । अधराहनुवद् रोधः तत् प्रतिच्छाययारुणम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: satyamarkakarāraktaṃ uttarāhanuvad‍ghanam adharāhanuvad rodhaḥ tat praticchāyayāruṇam

translation: (Another boy) “Really! The cloud reddened with, rays of the sun looks like his upper lip, while the earth grown, red through the reflection (of the cloud) looks like the lower lip.”

devanagari: प्रतिस्पर्धेते सृक्किभ्यां सव्यासव्ये नगोदरे । तुंगशृंगालयोऽप्येताः तद् दंष्ट्राभिश्च पश्यत ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: pratispardhete sṛkkibhyāṃ savyāsavye nagodare tuṃgaśṛṃgālayo

translation: (The third boy) “Look here! The mountain Caverns on its right as well as on the left are as if competing with the jaws; and the rows of lofty mountain-summits as it were vie with its fangs.

devanagari: आस्तृतायाम मार्गोऽयं रसनां प्रतिगर्जति । एषां अन्तर्गतं ध्वान्तं एतदप्यन्तः आननम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: āstṛtāyāma mārgo eṣāṃ antargataṃ dhvāntaṃ etadapyantaḥ ānanam
translation: The broad and long road is vying with its tongue, while the darkness pent up in between the peaks, emulates the darkness in the internal part of its mouth.

devanagari: दावोष्णखरवातोऽयं श्वासवद्‍भाति पश्यत । तद् दग्धसत्त्वदुर्गन्धोऽपि अन्तरामिषगन्धवत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: dāvoṣṇakharavāto tad dagdhasattvadurgandho
translation: Look here! The fierce blast of wind heated by forest fire feels like its breath, and the bad odour of animals burnt in that forest fire stinks like the flesh of animals in the stomach of a serpent.

devanagari: अस्मान्किमत्र ग्रसिता निविष्टा । नयं तथा चेद् बकवद् विनङ्क्ष्यति । क्षणादनेनेति बकार्युशन्मुखं । वीक्ष्योद्धसन्तः करताडनैर्ययुः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: asmānkimatra grasitā niviṣṭā nayaṃ tathā ced bakavad vinaṅkṣyati kṣaṇādaneneti bakāryuśanmukhaṃ vīkṣyoddhasantaḥ karatāḍanairyayuḥ

translation: “Will he swallow us all if we enter his mouth?[3] In that case, like Bakāsura, he will meet his death in a moment at the hands of Kṛṣṇa”. Saying so, and looking at the fascinating face of Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of Baka, they all entered (that mouth) laughing loudly and clapping their hands.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
v.l. viśantu sarve grasitāḥ kim asmān:
Let us all enter it. Let us see if he can devour us—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: इत्थं मिथोऽतथ्यं अतज्ज्ञभाषितं । श्रुत्वा विचिन्त्येत्यमृषा मृषायते । रक्षो विदित्वाखिलभूतहृत्स्थितः । स्वानां निरोद्धुं भगवान् मनो दध...
english_devnagari: itthaṃ mitho śrutvā vicintyetyamṛṣā mṛṣāyate rakṣo viditvākhilabhūtahṛtsthitaḥ svānāṃ niroddhuṃ bhagavān mano dadhe

translation: Hearing the mutual discussion—which was far removed from reality—of his friends who were ignorant of the factual state of things, Kṛṣṇa knew that that was really a demon simulating the form of a serpent, as the Lord is present in the hearts of all creatures. The Lord made up his mind to prevent his companions from entering the demon’s mouth.

devanagari: तावत् प्रविष्टास्तु असुरोदरान्तरं । परं न गीर्णाः शिशवः सवत्साः । प्रतीक्षमाणेन बकारिवेशनं । हतस्वकान्तस्मरणेन रक्षसा ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvat praviṣṭāstu asurodarāntaraṃ paraṃ na gīrṇāḥ śiśavaḥ savatsāḥ pratīkṣamāṇena bakāriveśanaṃ hatasvakāntasmaraṇena rakṣasā

translation: In the meanwhile, the children along with the calves entered into the bowels of that demon. But they were not swallowed up by that ogre who remembered the slaying of his dear ones, and was expecting the entry of the slayer of Baka viz. Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तान् वीक्ष्य कृष्णः सकलाभयप्रदो । ह्यनन्यनाथान् स्वकरादवच्युतान् । दीनांश्च मृत्योर्जठराग्निघासान् । घृणार्दितो दिष्टकृतेन विस्मितः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: tān vīkṣya kṛṣṇaḥ sakalābhayaprado hyananyanāthān svakarādavacyutān dīnāṃśca mṛtyorjaṭharāgnighāsān ghṛṇārdito diṣṭakṛtena vismitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[instrumental single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [instrumental single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa, who affords protection to all, was moved with compassion to see the helpless children and calves who had no other saviour, and who having slipped out of (the protective control of) his hands, had become the victims of the gastric fire of the demon. He was surprised[4] at the working of the Providence.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
VT. states that Kṛṣṇa was surprised at the powerful working of Providence, as it exercised its effect even in the presence of himself, in the case of his devotees who entered the mouth of Aghāsura despite his attempts to prevent them.

devanagari: कृत्यं किमत्रास्य खलस्य जीवनं । न वा अमीषां च सतां विहिंसनम् । द्वयं कथं स्यादिति संविचिन्त्य तत् । ज्ञात्वाविशत् तुण्डमशेषदृग्घरिः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: kṛtyaṃ kimatrāsya khalasya jīvanaṃ na vā amīṣāṃ ca satāṃ vihiṃsanam dvayaṃ kathaṃ syāditi saṃvicintya tat jñātvāviśat tuṇḍamaśeṣadṛgghariḥ

[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: “What should be done now? How both the objects viz. the termination of the life of this wicked (demon) and averting the death of these pious innocents, can be achieved?” Pondering over this problem, and knowing the expedient, the omniscient Hari entered his (Aghāsura’s) mouth.

devanagari: तदा घनच्छदा देवा भयाद् हाहेति चुक्रुशुः । जहृषुर्ये च कंसाद्याः कौणपास्त्वघबान्धवाः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā ghanacchadā devā bhayād hāheti cukruśuḥ jahṛṣurye ca kaṃsādyāḥ kauṇapāstvaghabāndhavāḥ
translation: At that instant, gods who were behind the curtain of clouds, shrieked in terror bewailing ‘Alas! Alas’, while the demonic friends of Aghāsura of whom Kaṃsa was prominent, exulted in joy.

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वा भगवान् कृष्णस्तु अव्ययः सार्भवत्सकम् । चूर्णीचिकीर्षोरात्मानं तरसा ववृधे गले ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvā bhagavān kṛṣṇastu avyayaḥ sārbhavatsakam cūrṇīcikīrṣorātmānaṃ tarasā vavṛdhe gale

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Hearing those (cries and shouts of joy), the Imperishable glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa instantly increased himself in huge dimensions into the throat of that demon who desired to crush to powder Kṛṣṇa along with children and calves.

devanagari: ततोऽतिकायस्य निरुद्धमार्गिणो । ह्युद्‍गीर्णदृष्टेः भ्रमतस्त्वितस्ततः । पूर्णोऽन्तरंगे पवनो निरुद्धो । मूर्धन् विनिष्पाट्य विनिर्गतो बह...
english_devnagari: tato hyud‍gīrṇadṛṣṭeḥ bhramatastvitastataḥ pūrṇo mūrdhan viniṣpāṭya vinirgato bahiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb]

translation: All the passage of breath of that huge-bodied monster was choked, and his eyes shot out of their sockets. He was writhing on all sides. His breath being completely obstructed in his lungs, forced its way out through an aperture in the crown of its head called Brahma randhra.

devanagari: तेनैव सर्वेषु बहिर्गतेषु । प्राणेषु वत्सान् सुहृदः परेतान् । दृष्ट्या स्वयोत्थाप्य तदन्वितः पुनः । वक्त्रान् मुकुन्दो भगवान् विनिर्ययौ...
english_devnagari: tenaiva sarveṣu bahirgateṣu prāṇeṣu vatsān suhṛdaḥ paretān dṛṣṭyā svayotthāpya tadanvitaḥ punaḥ vaktrān mukundo bhagavān viniryayau
translation: When all the vital breaths of the demon passed out that way, Lord Kṛṣṇa revived to life with his nectarine look, all his friends and calves who were as good as dead, and came out of the mouth of the demon again, along with them.

devanagari: पीनाहिभोगोत्थितमद्‍भुतं महत् । ज्योतिः स्वधाम्ना ज्वलयद् दिशो दश । प्रतीक्ष्य खेऽवस्थितमीशनिर्गमं । विवेश तस्मिन् मिषतां दिवौकसाम् ॥ ३...
english_devnagari: pīnāhibhogotthitamad‍bhutaṃ mahat jyotiḥ svadhāmnā jvalayad diśo daśa pratīkṣya khe viveśa tasmin miṣatāṃ divaukasām

translation: A mysterious and powerful light setting ablaze (as it were) all the ten directions, with its brilliant effulgence, rose from the huge body of the serpent. It waited in the firmament for the coming out of the Lord (from the serpent’s mouth) and (immediately) entered his person while all the residents of the celestial world simply looked on in amazement.

devanagari: ततोऽतिहृष्टाः स्वकृतोऽकृतार्हणं । पुष्पैः सुगा अप्सरसश्च नर्तनैः । गीतैः सुरा वाद्यधराश्च वाद्यकैः । स्तवैश्च विप्रा जयनिःस्वनैर्गणाः ...
english_devnagari: tato puṣpaiḥ sugā apsarasaśca nartanaiḥ gītaiḥ surā vādyadharāśca vādyakaiḥ stavaiśca viprā jayaniḥsvanairgaṇāḥ

translation: Overjoyed at the accomplishment of their purpose by Kṛṣṇa, the jubilant gods offered their worship by showering flowers, the celestial damsels, through dancing, Gandharvas, experts in vocal music, with singing, those well-versed, in instrumental music, by playing on their instruments, Brāhmaṇas (like Nārada) through singing hymns of praise, and his attendants and followers (like Garuḍa and others) with shouts of victory.

devanagari: तदद्‍भुतस्तोत्रसुवाद्यगीतिका । जयादिनैकोत्सव मङ्गलस्वनान् । श्रुत्वा स्वधाम्नोऽन्त्यज आगतोऽचिराद् । दृष्ट्वा महीशस्य जगाम विस्मयम् ॥ ३...
english_devnagari: tadad‍bhutastotrasuvādyagītikā jayādinaikotsava maṅgalasvanān śrutvā svadhāmno dṛṣṭvā mahīśasya jagāma vismayam

translation: Hearing in the vicinity of his abode (Satya Loka) the auspicious and joyous sounds of wonderful hymns of praise, instrumental and vocal music and shouts of victory, god Brahmā, immediately came to the spot and was wonder-struck at the glorious feat of the Lord.

devanagari: राजन् आजगरं चर्म शुष्कं वृन्दावनेऽद्‍भुतम् । व्रजौकसां बहुतिथं बभूवाक्रीडगह्वरम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: rājan ājagaraṃ carma śuṣkaṃ vṛndāvane vrajaukasāṃ bahutithaṃ babhūvākrīḍagahvaram

translation: Oh King! When the hide of that weird boa-constrictor became dry, it served for a long time as a hiding place in playing (like a big cave) to the residents of Vraja.

devanagari: एतत्कौमारजं कर्म हरेरात्माहिमोक्षणम् । मृत्योः पौगण्डके बाला दृष्ट्वोचुर्विस्मिता व्रजे ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: etatkaumārajaṃ karma harerātmāhimokṣaṇam mṛtyoḥ paugaṇḍake bālā dṛṣṭvocurvismitā vraje
translation: Witnessing the exploit of Hari, as a child of five years, in rescuing them from death (from Aghāsura) and the demon-serpent from death (involved in rebirth in Saṃsāra), the cowherd boys of Vraja told to each other of it in his sixth year with equal astonishment.

devanagari: नैतद् विचित्रं मनुजार्भमायिनः । परावराणां परमस्य वेधसः । अघोऽपि यत्स्पर्शनधौतपातकः । प्रापात्मसाम्यं त्वसतां सुदुर्लभम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: naitad vicitraṃ manujārbhamāyinaḥ parāvarāṇāṃ paramasya vedhasaḥ agho prāpātmasāmyaṃ tvasatāṃ sudurlabham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: That even a demon like Agha should get his sins absolved by his touch, and should get absorbed in him—an attainment extremely difficult for the unrighteous, is not to be wondered at in the case of Hari who has assumed through his Māyā, the form of a human child, but who is (really) the Creator of all beings, great and small.

devanagari: सकृद्यदङ्गप्रतिमान्तराहिता । मनोमयी भागवतीं ददौ गतिम् । स एव नित्यात्मसुखानुभूत्यभि । व्युदस्तमायोऽन्तर्गतो हि किं पुनः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: sakṛdyadaṅgapratimāntarāhitā manomayī bhāgavatīṃ dadau gatim sa eva nityātmasukhānubhūtyabhi vyudastamāyo

translation: Even if the semblance of the personality of the Lord is once installed in one’s heart in meditation, it leads to the attainment of divine position. What indeed would he not give to Aghāsura in whom he had actually entered, as he had warded off Māyā by the eternal realization of the bliss of his Divine self.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Here ends the XIII Chapter in Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । इत्थं द्विजा यादवदेवदत्तः । श्रुत्वा स्वरातुश्चरितं विचित्रम् । पप्रच्छ भूयोऽपि तदेव पुण्यं । वैयासकिं यन्निगृहीतचेताः ...

translation:
Sūta said:
Oh Śaunaka and other Brāhmaṇas! Having heard this wonderful story of his saviour, king Parīkṣit, who was a gift (whose life was protected in womb) by Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Yādavas, found his heart fascinated with it, and again requested Śuka, the Son of Vyāsa, to narrate the same sacred story about him.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । ब्रह्मन् कालान्तरकृतं तत्कालीनं कथं भवेत् । यत्कौमारे हरिकृतं जगुः पौगण्डकेऽर्भकाः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca brahman kālāntarakṛtaṃ tatkālīnaṃ kathaṃ bhavet yatkaumāre harikṛtaṃ jaguḥ paugaṇḍake

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) submitted[6]
Oh Brāhmaṇa Sage! You said just now that what was done by Hari in his childhood stage of five years, was sung by the cowherd boys in the sixth year (in the paugaṇḍa stage)[7]. Now could an act done at a distant time, can be regarded as done in the present time.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Ch. XIII in Bhāgavata Candrikā begins here.

Footnote [7]:
The following are names of different stages in life: childhood Kaumāra to the end of the 5th year, boyhood (Paugaṇḍa) to the end of the 10th year Adolescence (Kiśora. to the end of the 15th year, Youth after the 15th year (Yauvana).

devanagari: तद् ब्रूहि मे महायोगिन् परं कौतूहलं गुरो । नूनमेतद्धरेरेव माया भवति नान्यथा ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tad brūhi me mahāyogin paraṃ kautūhalaṃ guro nūnametaddharereva māyā bhavati nānyathā
translation: Be pleased to narrate to me, Oh Great Yogin, as I feel great curiosity about it. How can it be possible unless it is due to the deluding power (Māyā) of Hari, Oh preceptor.

devanagari: वयं धन्यतमा लोके गुरोऽपि क्षत्रबन्धवः । यत् पिबामो मुहुस्त्वत्तः पुण्यं कृष्णकथामृतम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ dhanyatamā loke guro yat pibāmo muhustvattaḥ puṇyaṃ kṛṣṇakathāmṛtam

translation: Though we are (after all) ordinary Kṣatriyas weare the blessed-most persons in this world, inasmuch as we can repeatedly drink the hallowing nectar in the form of stories of Lord Kṛṣṇa from you.

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । इत्थं स्म पृष्टः स तु बादरायणिः । तत्स्मारितानन्तहृताखिलेन्द्रियः । कृच्छ्रात् पुनर्लब्धबहिर्दृशिः शनैः । प्रत्याह तं भ...
english_devnagari: śrīsūta uvāca itthaṃ sma pṛṣṭaḥ sa tu bādarāyaṇiḥ tatsmāritānantahṛtākhilendriyaḥ kṛcchrāt punarlabdhabahirdṛśiḥ śanaiḥ pratyāha taṃ bhāgavatottamottama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Sūta said:
Oh the most excellent devotee of the Lord! When thus queried, Śuka, the son of Bādarāyaṇa who lost his senses (i.e. consciousness), due to his immersion in contemplation about Kṛṣṇa when reminded of the Lord by Parīkṣit’s question, slowly regained his consciousness of the external world (with difficulty), and began to narrate.
